name: SLICC
philosophy: SLICC — the self-licking ice cream cone. A browser-native AI agent that lives in your browser, automating the web apps you already use. Client-side only: no server, no API keys, nothing to approve.

variants.name: Cone mark (on dark)
variants.usage: Primary nav + favicon on the default dark surface

logo.clearSpace: Keep clear space equal to the scoop radius around the mark.
donts: Don't recolor the mark outside the brand palette
Don't separate the scoop from the cone

primary.name: Ice Cream Cream
primary.role: Light surface — use-cases, install, different sections

secondary.name: Hairline
secondary.role: Outline buttons, borders, dividers on dark

borderRadius.usage: Primary CTAs and tile buttons use 8px; compact pill buttons drop to 6px. Never fully round — the brand wants a software-tool feel, not a consumer-app pill.

patterns.name: Primary (Cherry)
patterns.style: background #E0244F, text #FFFFFF, 2px solid #E0244F, 8px radius, 14px 28px padding, Space Mono 400

patterns.name: Secondary (ghost on dark)
patterns.style: transparent bg, text #F0F0F0, 2px solid #333, 8px radius, 14px 28px padding, Space Grotesk 400

patterns.name: Tile (install target)
patterns.style: background rgba(255,255,255,0.04), text #F0F0F0, 1px solid #333, 8px radius, 10px 18px padding, Space Grotesk 400

patterns.name: Video chrome
patterns.style: background rgba(255,255,255,0.15), text #FFF, 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.25), 6px radius, 10px 22px padding, Space Mono 400

buttons.dualCTA: Hero pairs primary Cherry fill with a ghost outline — 'Get SLICC' alongside 'See it work'.

motifs.name: Strike-through rewrite
motifs.description: Headline words are crossed out and replaced inline — e.g. 'An [ice cream] AI agent that lives in your browser.' The crossed phrase reads first, the real one lands second.
motifs.usage: Hero display headlines only. One rewrite per headline, max.

motifs.name: Ice-cream-meets-terminal vocabulary
motifs.description: Dev-shop copy smuggled in through dessert metaphors — 'the self-licking ice cream cone', 'Under the waffle hood', 'chmod +x everything', 'Here's where it gets weird.'
motifs.usage: Section headers and eyebrow callouts. Don't force it into every heading — it loses impact.

motifs.name: Light / dark section alternation
motifs.description: Long page alternates dark Cone Black sections with bright Ice Cream Cream sections — the rhythm is part of the identity.
motifs.usage: Use dark for atmosphere + demos, cream for feature walkthroughs and install.

motifs.name: Tool-logo wall
motifs.description: Grid of recognizable product logos (GitHub, Figma, Slack, Notion, …) as a 'works with everything' signal.
motifs.usage: Under hero, and again under 'Bring your own tokens'. Logos in neutral monochrome, never colored.

motifs.name: Low-opacity body ink
motifs.description: Hero subhead renders at ~0.85 opacity on #F0F0F0 — soft, not pure white, on near-black.
motifs.usage: Lede copy + supporting lines in dark sections. Keep pure #F0F0F0 for primary body.

heading.usage: All display + section headlines. Weight 300 for the hero at ~75px; 700 for compact section H2s.

body.usage: Running copy. 18px on dark, 22px for hero lede. Body runs at ~0.85 opacity on dark surfaces; full opacity on cream.

accent.usage: Hand-drawn marker accent for informal scrawls — strike-through rewrites, arrows, one-word jokes. Use sparingly, never for body or CTAs.

eyebrow.usage: Technical labels, badges, and section eyebrows where a code-like voice fits.

button.usage: Primary CTAs use Space Mono to echo the terminal side of the brand.

rules: Display headlines at weight 300 — the brand leans light, not bold, on the hero.
Primary CTA label is always in Space Mono; secondary/ghost buttons in Space Grotesk.
Body copy softens to ~0.85 opacity on dark surfaces. Full opacity on cream.
Marker accent (Permanent Marker) is reserved — one scrawl per section max.

photography.style: Minimal, product-forward screenshots and silent-video demos. No stock photography, no people. When imagery is needed it's the UI itself — terminal, browser, app chrome — framed clean on Cone Black or Ice Cream Cream.
rules: Lead with a product video or UI capture, not a photograph.
Crop tight to the action — no decorative browser chrome unless it tells the story.
Keep captures rendered at 2× for crisp display; subtle 8px rounding on frames.
donts: No stock 'diverse team at laptop' photography.
No humans, hands on keyboards, or office interiors.
No gradient backdrops behind product shots — flat brand surfaces only.
photography.social: Square crops of the cone mark on Cherry or Cone Black; short silent loops of the agent doing a task. Caption in Space Mono.

voice.character: A dev tool with a sugar high. Technically precise, winkingly self-aware, allergic to enterprise-speak. Says what it does, then makes a joke about saying what it does.

do.text: An ice-cream AI agent that lives in your browser.
do.context: Strike-through rewrite lands the joke and the pitch at the same time.

do.text: SLICC — the self-licking ice cream cone.
do.context: The tagline *is* the joke. No explanation offered.
Section header for 'how it works'. Terminal idea dressed as dessert.
Permissions section. Three words, one idea, zero fluff.
Smart eyebrow before an unusual capability — sets expectations without selling.
Tiny mechanical metaphor that explains the product in six words.

do.text: No server, no API keys, nothing to approve.
do.context: Rhythmic triplet. Each clause gets shorter. Ends on the zinger.

dont.text: Revolutionize your workflow with next-generation agentic AI.
dont.context: Every word is a buzzword. SLICC would never.

dont.text: SLICC empowers enterprise teams to unlock productivity.
dont.context: 'Empowers' and 'unlock' are on the permanent banlist.
The brand is deadpan. It's not excited; it's busy.

dont.text: An intelligent automation platform for the modern browser.
dont.context: Generic category copy. No joke, no specifics, no voice.

rules: No exclamation points. The brand is dry.
Earn every dessert metaphor — don't sprinkle them on every heading.
Short clauses. Rhythmic triplets are on-brand: 'no server, no API keys, nothing to approve.'
If a line could appear on any other SaaS homepage, rewrite it.

tone.description: Tone stays deadpan across contexts; only the density of jokes changes. Hero and section headers earn one gag each; docs and install steps go fully straight.

writingGoals.description: Make a very technical product feel approachable with one small joke per screen — never two.
Explain what SLICC does in concrete mechanical terms: 'your browser sessions, its hands.'
Assume the reader is a developer. Skip the setup; land the point.

cleverVsClear.clever: Hero headlines, section eyebrows, taglines. One gag per surface, then move on.
cleverVsClear.clear: Install steps, 'bring your own tokens', any copy that answers 'will this break my machine?'. Jokes off.

contentPillars.name: Browser-native automation
contentPillars.description: Everything SLICC does happens inside your browser — automating web apps you're already signed into.

contentPillars.name: Client-side trust
contentPillars.description: No server, no keys in the cloud. Bring your own tokens; SLICC doesn't see them.

contentPillars.name: Parallel agents
contentPillars.description: Coordinate multiple agents across tabs. The weird, interesting half of the product.

personas.name: The Tinkering Developer
personas.description: Senior-ish engineer who has tried three agent frameworks this quarter and is tired of spinning up servers. Runs everything locally if they can.
personas.motto: If it needs a server, it's already too much.

stats.description: API keys sent to a third party
of the agent runs in the browser

extraction.method: Playwright (Chromium, 1440×900, 2x DPR) — computed styles + fullpage screenshot
